intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll process the first sample (Patient 145, Day 0) with scanpy, following the standard QC → normalization → clustering → visualization pipeline.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Now let me present the plan for processing the first sample:

input.title: scanpy QC & clustering for GSE192391 Patient 145 Day 0
input.summary: Standard single-cell QC, normalization, and clustering on the first sample (7,532 cells)
input.rationale: Establish the baseline cell-type composition and quality of the day-0 sample before longitudinal comparison

steps.title: Load and inspect the first sample
steps.description: Extract GSM5746259 (Patient 145 Day 0, 7,532 cells) from the combined object and calculate QC metrics (n_genes, pct_mito, pct_ribo)

steps.title: QC filtering
steps.description: Filter cells with n_genes ≥ 200 and pct_mito < 0.20 (standard thresholds); report cells retained

steps.title: Identify highly variable genes (HVG)
steps.description: sc.pp.highly_variable_genes with default flavor, plot dispersion

steps.title: PCA and Leiden clustering
steps.description: PCA on HVGs, compute neighbors (k=15), run Leiden clustering (resolution=1.0)

steps.title: UMAP visualization and marker genes
steps.description: Compute UMAP on PC space; visualize clusters; rank marker genes per cluster (t-test)

assumptions: Mitochondrial genes identified by 'MT-' prefix in HGNC symbols
Resolution=1.0 for Leiden as a reasonable starting point
